content: The grant proposal application requires a title page with the research title, PI and co-investigators information (name, institute), research authority, and date of application. Signatures of the PI and the Research Authority are required.
Abstracts (English, Hebrew, and Layman's term) must include background and aim, methods, expected results, importance to medicine, and five keywords. Limited to one page each.

The research plan, in English, should be up to 10 pages for proposals (up to 3 pages for pre-proposals), excluding references. It must contain scientific background, research question, study design, detailed plan, methods including sample size justification and available equipment, preliminary results, schematic representation with responsibilities and time scheme, and a time estimate for each stage.
Up to 15 references should be included (First Author, Full title, Journal, Volume, Page, Year).

The budget should be described for the whole research duration. Salaries to PI and CoI, permanent equipment (including computers and software), clinics charges, and travels abroad are not eligible costs. Overhead is capped at 10% of the final sum.
Provide justification if the project is supported by another grant or if a similar application was made.
A curriculum vitae form must be filled out for each applicant, including education, employment, and active research grants and contracts.
Collaboration letters are required from collaborators consenting to contribute to the research, indicating their part in it, as B&W, low resolution PDF files attached to the electronic form.

If applicable, include bio-ethics approvals: Helsinki approval for research on human subjects, Institutional Committee for Animal Research approval, Institutional Safety Committee approval, and/or approval from the Ministry of Education.
Provide the names and email addresses of at least 5 experts in the field, avoiding reviewers with conflicts of interest.

The proposal should be compiled in the following order: printed copy of the complete electronic form of the "Request for Research Funding" including signatures of all research partners and the Research Authority, abstracts in Hebrew, English, and layman's terms, research plan, budget, CVs of all researchers, collaboration letters, bio-ethics approvals, and a list of potential reviewers.
cfp_subject: This call for proposals focuses on funding research in various areas of health and medicine. It requires detailed research plans, budgets, and ethical approvals, targeting researchers and institutions capable of conducting in-depth studies.
